cards.title: Claim-Ledger Security Triage
cards.use_when: A scanner dumped a pile of findings and you want only the ones backed by a runnable repro.
cards.verify: Every accepted finding has a PoC that fails before the patch and passes after; an independent verifier re-ran each.

cards.title: Flaky-Test Quarantine Warden
cards.use_when: Flaky tests are eroding trust in CI and you want a loop to detect, quarantine, and file them.
cards.verify: Each quarantined test failed intermittently across N reruns; the suite's flake rate trends down.

cards.title: Overnight Code Review (governed)
cards.use_when: You want an agent to review an open-PR queue continuously, without it re-reviewing unchanged PRs forever.
cards.verify: No PR has changed head SHA since the last pass; cost stays under the nightly cap.

cards.title: Reproduce-Before-You-Fix
cards.use_when: A regression needs fixing and you want to forbid touching source until the bug is reliably reproduced.
cards.verify: A test reproduces the bug on 10/10 runs before any source edit; the fix makes it green.

cards.title: Two Loops, One Repo (coordinated)
cards.use_when: You want two agents (e.g. a refactor loop and a docs loop) working the same repo without clobbering each other.
cards.verify: Zero merge conflicts, clobbers, or reverts across a run; every edit held a lease.

cards.title: Ad-Spend Reconciliation
cards.use_when: You need messy invoices reconciled against a bank export to a hard zero-variance gate.
cards.verify: The reconciliation workbook shows variance of zero and zero unmatched rows.

cards.title: Log-Anomaly Patrol
cards.use_when: You want a security-camera loop that watches logs for new error signatures and opens an issue once per signature.
cards.verify: Each opened issue maps to an error signature not seen in the prior window; no duplicate issues.

cards.title: SaaS Spend-Audit Circuit-Breaker
cards.use_when: You want a recurring audit of SaaS/cloud spend that flags anomalies but can't run up its own bill.
cards.verify: Each flagged line exceeds its baseline by the configured threshold; the audit itself stays under a cost cap.

cards.title: Changelog From Commits
cards.use_when: You want a release changelog drafted only from the actual commit/PR history, with every entry traceable.
cards.verify: Every changelog line cites a real commit or PR in the range; no entry is unsourced.

cards.title: Deliverability Rescue (climb to a gate)
cards.use_when: An email/landing page must clear a spam/quality score and you want a writer-vs-scorer loop to climb to it.
cards.verify: A separate scorer rates the final draft at or above the gate on a frozen rubric.

cards.title: Docs-Freshness Patrol
cards.use_when: You want a patrol that flags docs whose code examples or referenced APIs have drifted from the codebase.
cards.verify: Each flagged doc references a symbol/path that no longer exists or a snippet that no longer compiles.

cards.title: Claim-Verify Research Brief
cards.use_when: You want a research brief where every claim is independently verified before it is allowed to stay.
cards.verify: Each surviving claim has >= 2 independent supporting sources; unverified claims were cut or flagged.

cards.title: Competitive-Intel Digest
cards.use_when: You want a scheduled digest of changes to a watchlist of competitor pages, with each claim linked to its source.
cards.verify: Every digest item links to the specific source page and quotes the changed text; nothing is unsourced.

cards.title: RFP / Security Questionnaire Pack
cards.use_when: You must answer a long questionnaire and every answer has to cite an approved source or be left as a gap.
cards.verify: Every answered row cites a source line from the approved binder; unsupported claims are marked GAP, not invented.

cards.title: Messy-CSV Normalizer
cards.use_when: You have messy CSVs (mixed formats, currencies, dates, encodings) to normalize to a target schema.
cards.verify: Every output row conforms to the target schema; a round-trip check reproduces source totals.

cards.title: Data-Quality Gate Patrol
cards.use_when: You want a patrol that checks a dataset/table against quality rules and blocks or flags on violations.
cards.verify: Every flagged row violates a stated rule; a clean pass reports zero violations against the rule set.

cards.title: Schema-Migration Backfill
cards.use_when: You're backfilling a new column/table and need every affected row migrated with a verifiable invariant.
cards.verify: Post-migration, the invariant holds for 100% of affected rows and counts match the pre-migration plan.
